key_criteria_names: Experience with US Laws and Regulations
Ability to Work Cross-Functionally and Influence Across a Global Business
Ability to Work in a Hands-On Entrepreneurial Environment

scope_requirements.Geography: Global oversight across US, Mexico, UK, Germany, China, India

scope_match.candidate_actual: US-based (Buffalo, NY); oversees US export control compliance for 30+ international industrial subsidiaries
scope_match.role_requirement: Global oversight across US, Mexico, UK, Germany, China, India

scope_match.candidate_actual: Aerospace and industrial manufacturing (85-90% industrial, 10-15% military/defense)

key_criteria.name: Strong Export Compliance Experience
key_criteria.evidence: Proficient across ITAR, EAR, and OFAC with hands-on DDTC licensing, classifications, and SAP DECCS experience. Served as co-Business Process Owner for multi-year SAP GTS implementation at **Moog Inc.** and holds Empowered Official designation under ITAR, with authority to sign export licences and make final compliance determinations.

key_criteria.name: Experience with US Laws and Regulations
key_criteria.evidence: Confirmed expertise across EAR, ITAR, and OFAC with direct experience using US Government licensing tools and sanctioned party screening lists at **Moog Inc.** Responsible for ensuring compliance policies are updated in line with evolving US trade laws, and acts as strategic advisor on export matters across the organisation.

key_criteria.name: Aerospace and Defense Experience
key_criteria.evidence: Portfolio at **Moog Inc.** is weighted 85–90% industrial applications (power generation, paper mills) with only 10–15% military/defence work. Holds Empowered Official designation under ITAR but interview did not reveal depth in aerospace-specific classification systems or FAR/DFAR requirements central to Doncasters’ focus.

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work Cross-Functionally and Influence Across a Global Business
key_criteria.evidence: Primary US regulatory contact across 30+ international industrial subsidiaries at **Moog Inc.**, working through trade compliance champions at each foreign entity without direct authority. Regularly advises C-suite and executive teams, and provides guidance to other operating entities including Space & Defence, Aircraft Group, and corporate purchasing.

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work in a Hands-On Entrepreneurial Environment
key_criteria.evidence: Currently operates as individual contributor after specialist role beneath her went unfilled, handling both strategic and tactical compliance work at **Moog Inc.** Progressed from accepting compliance role after single training session to leading major system implementations and building out UK/Germany compliance programs.

compensation.expected_total: $150,000 base salary. Katie previously mentioned $140K via LinkedIn but clarified in interview that she would be "more apt to leave at least if we were closer to like 150" given she is not actively looking and values current company culture
compensation.flexibility: Katie demonstrated flexibility by revising expectations upward from previously mentioned $140K to preferred $150K base, acknowledging this reflects her passive candidate status and satisfaction with current culture. Recruiter confirmed Doncasters can "realistically do a base of 150 plus probably about a 10% bonus" which Katie acknowledged positively

our_take.text: Katie brings solid hands-on US export compliance expertise and has successfully built international compliance programs in a lean environment, which maps well to the Doncasters structure and UK/Germany build-out objective. However, her 85-90% industrial vs. 10-15% defense experience mix is inverted from what Doncasters needs in a primarily aerospace role, and we should validate whether her ITAR classification depth extends beyond the lower-complexity industrial applications that appear to dominate her current portfolio.

candidates.flash_summary: Katie Connacher is Trade Compliance Manager at Moog Inc., based in Buffalo, New York, where she has spent her entire 12+ year professional career within the Industrial Group.

scope_match.candidate_actual: US-based (Dallas, TX); remote individual contributor managing multi-site US operations
scope_match.role_requirement: Global oversight across US, Mexico, UK, Germany, China, India

scope_match.candidate_actual: Reports to Senior Manager of Global Trade within legal function
Aerospace and defense contractor (Raytheon — electronics and infrared technologies)

key_criteria.name: Strong Export Compliance Experience
key_criteria.evidence: 15 years of deep US export compliance expertise across **Raytheon**, **L3 Technologies**, and **Elbit Systems of America**. Manages all licensing start-to-finish including ITAR and Commerce classifications, and serves as Part 130 lead at Raytheon handling DDTC processes for electronics and infrared technologies.

key_criteria.name: Experience with US Laws and Regulations
key_criteria.evidence: Daily application of ITAR and EAR regulations at **Raytheon** under heightened government monitoring, with experience across DDTC processes, BIS regulations, ECCN and HTS Schedule B classifications. 15 years managing compliance across major defence contractors demonstrates the regulatory depth required.

key_criteria.name: Aerospace and Defense Experience
key_criteria.evidence: Deep aerospace and defence expertise spanning 15+ years across **Raytheon**, **Elbit Systems of America**, and **L3 Technologies**. At Raytheon, manages compliance for ITAR-controlled electronics and infrared technologies. At Elbit, managed export compliance under FOCI for a US-incorporated Israeli-owned defence contractor.

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work Cross-Functionally and Influence Across a Global Business
key_criteria.evidence: Works with senior leadership across regions at **Raytheon**, providing compliance training and education across global operations. Managed multi-site operations at **Open Systems** (Fort Worth and Florida) and **DRS**. Current role primarily US-focused; international site management depth not explicitly demonstrated.

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work in a Hands-On Entrepreneurial Environment
key_criteria.evidence: Stated preference for being "hands-on" and has worked remotely from home for 9 years at **Raytheon**, managing 12 concurrent programmes as an individual contributor. Entire 15-year career within large defence contractors, which may present adjustment to Doncasters’ mid-sized manufacturing environment.

compensation.expected_total: $125,000 base salary (stated as "I would love to go to 125")
compensation.flexibility: Mary stated a clear target of $125,000 base salary representing approximately 15% increase from current $109,000 base. She expressed this as an aspiration ("would love to") rather than a firm requirement, suggesting some flexibility. The Doncasters range of $125,000-$140,000 base plus up to 20% bonus aligns well with her expectations and would represent meaningful total compensation increase from her current approximately $116,000 total cash.

our_take.text: Strong technical fit with deep ITAR/EAR expertise, though entire career has been within large defense contractors rather than mid-sized manufacturing environments.

candidates.flash_summary: Mary Lund is a Principal Analyst Global Trade at Raytheon, based in the Dallas area, where she has worked remotely for approaching 9 years with 15+ years of deep ITAR/EAR technical expertise.

scope_match.candidate_actual: US-based (Seattle, WA); supports global operations in China, Canada, UK, and Australia
scope_match.role_requirement: Global oversight across US, Mexico, UK, Germany, China, India

scope_match.candidate_actual: Aerospace and defense manufacturing (Boeing — 737 commercial, fabrication division)

key_criteria.name: Strong Export Compliance Experience
key_criteria.evidence: 5–6 years of dedicated export compliance experience at **Boeing** with deep proficiency in BIS licensing, particularly for titanium and aluminium bar exports. Familiar with SnapR, AES, DDTC, and DECCS systems. ITAR/DDTC licensing experience is limited — completed one DFT5 licence and describes ITAR as not her primary focus.

key_criteria.name: Experience with US Laws and Regulations
key_criteria.evidence: Working knowledge of ITAR, EAR, and DDTC at **Boeing**, including experience under State Department consent agreements and collaboration with special compliance officers. Participated in auditing and procedure updates to move compliance programmes from grey areas to structured procedures.

key_criteria.name: Aerospace and Defense Experience
key_criteria.evidence: 14+ years of aerospace industry experience exclusively at **Boeing**, working across commercial airplane programmes including the 737 and supporting fabrication divisions. Has worked on the 767-KC-46A military airplane programme but acknowledges being "not the heaviest on ITAR" compared to dedicated military counterparts.

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work Cross-Functionally and Influence Across a Global Business
key_criteria.evidence: Supports **Boeing**’s global operations across the Zhoushan China delivery centre and fabrication sites in Canada, UK, and Australia. Previous procurement role required translating complex technical requirements between engineers and suppliers — directly applicable to influencing operational staff without direct authority.

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work in a Hands-On Entrepreneurial Environment
key_criteria.evidence: Currently operates as individual contributor at **Boeing** and describes the role as hands-on, expressing strong interest in the Doncasters opportunity to be "in the middle of things and having a real impact." All experience within Boeing’s large corporate infrastructure; transition to mid-sized environment remains untested.

compensation.expected_total: $150,000 - $175,000 total compensation; subsequently indicated flexibility to accept $140,000 - $150,000 base + 10% variable bonus
compensation.flexibility: Lauren initially stated expectation of $150-175k total comp but when informed this was higher than the target range of $140-150k base + 10% bonus, she confirmed "that would be fine by me," demonstrating meaningful flexibility and no compensation-related deal-breakers.

our_take.text: We believe Lauren presents as an articulate and well-spoken professional with solid BIS licensing expertise, though her ITAR experience is admittedly lighter—she has completed a DFT5 license but acknowledges this is not her primary strength compared to colleagues dedicated to military programs.

candidates.flash_summary: Lauren Sakahara is a Trade Control Specialist - Export at Boeing, based in the Seattle area, where she has spent 14 years 3 months with deep BIS licensing expertise supporting global manufacturing operations.

scope_match.candidate_actual: US-based (Grand Prairie, TX); manages two US sites with global reporting to Paris HQ
scope_match.role_requirement: Global oversight across US, Mexico, UK, Germany, China, India

scope_match.candidate_actual: Reports to CEO/VP of subsidiary with bi-weekly reporting to global HQ in Paris
Aerospace and defense manufacturing (Safran — military defense contracts and civil/commercial aircraft)

key_criteria.name: Strong Export Compliance Experience
key_criteria.evidence: 15+ years of deep US export compliance expertise, currently handling DDTC licensing for defence contracts at **Safran Power Units** including missile technology requiring specialised ATF licences. Previously served as Empowered Official for ITAR and EAR at **Sellmark Corporation**, managing all licensing for products imported from Asia and Europe.

key_criteria.name: Experience with US Laws and Regulations
key_criteria.evidence: Direct DDTC licensing experience and functional adherence to ITAR, EAR, FTR, OFAC, and C-TPAT at **Safran Power Units**. Holds ATF Responsible Person certification from **Sellmark** for maintaining firearms inventory. Manages denied parties screening and licence determination across all regulatory frameworks.

key_criteria.name: Aerospace and Defense Experience
key_criteria.evidence: Manages compliance for military/defence contracts including target drones and cruise missiles at **Safran Power Units** (Grand Prairie), plus civil/commercial aircraft parts (San Diego). 10+ years at **Airbus Helicopters** maintaining ITAR, EAR, and Export Control Reform compliance with USML/CCL classification expertise.

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work Cross-Functionally and Influence Across a Global Business
key_criteria.evidence: Operates across every department at **Safran Power Units** with weekly meetings with CEO, COO, VPs, and executive team. Reports bi-weekly to global compliance head in Paris. Manages cross-departmental challenges from supply chain to customs. Global experience primarily US-focused with reporting rather than direct oversight of non-US sites.

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work in a Hands-On Entrepreneurial Environment
key_criteria.evidence: Describes current role at **Safran Power Units** as "very hands-on," managing different levels of operational complexity across departments. Led rapid scaling response during tariff crisis. Expressed enthusiasm for smaller companies where you can "effectuate change quickly" without red tape and welcomed starting as sole compliance team member.

compensation.flexibility: Not discussed in detail. The candidate confirmed the $120K-140K range aligns with her expectations, and the recruiter confirmed this matches the role's salary range, indicating no apparent compensation barriers.

our_take.text: We believe this candidate presents a compelling profile with deep aerospace/defense compliance expertise and demonstrated ability to operate independently across geographically dispersed operations. Her enthusiasm for the entrepreneurial environment and comfort starting as a sole compliance function owner aligns well with Doncasters' hands-on culture, though we should validate her UK/Germany regulatory familiarity and CMMC/FAR-DFAR experience in follow-up discussions.

candidates.flash_summary: Veaseleti Lupeheke is currently Trade Compliance Manager at Safran Power Units, overseeing trade compliance across two US sites with 15+ years of aerospace/defense compliance expertise.

candidates.location: St. Louis metro area (residence in Illinois, office in St. Louis)

scope_match.candidate_actual: US-based (St. Louis metro); single-site focus with some international travel
scope_match.role_requirement: Global oversight across US, Mexico, UK, Germany, China, India

scope_match.candidate_actual: Reports to Senior Director of Trade Compliance (under VP of Trade Compliance, legal department)
Defense manufacturing (Leonardo DRS — land systems, naval electronics); previously Boeing (F-15 platform)

key_criteria.name: Strong Export Compliance Experience
key_criteria.evidence: Current role at **Leonardo DRS** involves extensive ITAR/EAR licensing, classification, and operational compliance including auditing EEI filings and managing export documentation. At **Boeing**, focused predominantly on F-15 platform licensing with MLA and TAA agreement management across Saudi Arabia, Singapore, and Japan.

key_criteria.name: Experience with US Laws and Regulations
key_criteria.evidence: Daily application of ITAR and EAR regulations at **Leonardo DRS** including classification, CFR compliance, and record-keeping across defence programmes. US customs expertise from **Bunge** and **Eastman Chemical** (Category 14 ITAR controlled chemicals). CMMC and FAR/DFAR familiarity not explicitly confirmed.

key_criteria.name: Aerospace and Defense Experience
key_criteria.evidence: Manages ITAR/EAR compliance for mission equipment packages on defence platforms including Striker, JLTV, and Abrams at **Leonardo DRS**. 4+ years at **Boeing** on the F-15 platform with deep aerospace-specific licensing. Experience concentrated in land systems and fighter aircraft rather than Doncasters’ casting/forging focus.

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work Cross-Functionally and Influence Across a Global Business
key_criteria.evidence: Spends ~50% of time at **Leonardo DRS** fielding questions and providing guidance to internal business units without direct authority. Currently in Munich establishing Ukraine partnership trade compliance frameworks, demonstrating international stakeholder engagement. Supports primarily one site; Doncasters requires simultaneous multi-country influence.

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work in a Hands-On Entrepreneurial Environment
key_criteria.evidence: Explicitly confirmed entrepreneurial approach at **Leonardo DRS**, where manager gave full autonomy to establish processes and "make it work" without rigid structure. Left **Boeing** specifically seeking a more hands-on, multi-hat role. Characterises current work as operational and tactical — comfortable with ad-hoc problem-solving.

compensation.expected_total: Approximately $154,000 total package (10% increase above current $140,000)
compensation.flexibility: Niklas stated he is seeking "anything kind of 10% higher than that probably would be ideal" when referencing his current $140,000 total package, indicating this is a target rather than a rigid requirement. He did not express any deal-breakers or inflexibility on compensation structure.

our_take.text: We believe Niklas represents a technically strong candidate with the right regulatory expertise and entrepreneurial mindset, though his platform-specific defense background may require some adaptation to the secondary supplier precision manufacturing context at Doncasters.

candidates.flash_summary: Niklas G is a Senior Export Manager at Leonardo DRS, managing all ITAR/EAR licensing, classifications, and export documentation for the land systems division with prior Boeing F-15 platform experience.

scope_match.candidate_actual: US-based (Bellwood, IL); manages global compliance across US, Canada, Europe, and Asia
scope_match.role_requirement: Global oversight across US, Mexico, UK, Germany, China, India

scope_match.candidate_actual: Aerospace and defense manufacturing ($4B+ publicly traded — Woodward, Inc.)
Team leadership role managing compliance for entire aerospace segment

key_criteria.name: Strong Export Compliance Experience
key_criteria.evidence: Manages all DDTC and Department of Commerce licensing at **Woodward Inc.** including manufacturing licence agreements and technical assistance agreements for defence articles. Classified 500,000+ aircraft parts at **Kellstrom Aerospace** and leads enterprise classification governance ensuring consistent ECCN/USML determinations across product lines.

key_criteria.name: Experience with US Laws and Regulations
key_criteria.evidence: Manages compliance across ITAR, EAR, and DDTC licensing at **Woodward Inc.**, overseeing foreign visitor programmes under technology control plans and directing internal audits with corrective action plans in coordination with Legal and Audit. Familiar with UK classification system, noting alignment with EAR.

key_criteria.name: Aerospace and Defense Experience
key_criteria.evidence: ~15 years of aerospace and defence experience. At **Woodward Inc.** ($4B+), manages trade compliance for the entire aerospace segment — the largest part of the business. Manages manufacturing licence agreements for defence programmes and technology control plans restricting military technology access to foreign persons.

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work Cross-Functionally and Influence Across a Global Business
key_criteria.evidence: Maintains constant dialogue with foreign affiliates across UK, India, Germany, Poland, Bulgaria, Canada, and France at **Woodward Inc.** Raises compliance awareness across commercial ops, sales, supply chain, shipping, and logistics teams, and advises executive leadership on export-controlled transactions and risk exposure.

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work in a Hands-On Entrepreneurial Environment
key_criteria.evidence: Built Export Management Compliance Programme from inception as individual contributor over 6.5 years at **NB Coatings**. Has worked in contract and project-based roles demonstrating adaptability. Current role at **Woodward Inc.** managing a team in a $4B company is more resourced than Doncasters’ lean IC structure.

compensation.expected_total: $150,000 base with 10% flexibility (from enhanced notes; not confirmed in transcript)
compensation.flexibility: Not discussed in transcript; enhanced notes indicate openness to 10% flexibility but this requires confirmation

our_take.text: We believe Michael represents an excellent fit for this role, bringing the precise blend of aerospace-focused US export compliance expertise, global stakeholder management, and M&A integration experience that Doncasters requires. His hands-on program-building background and current enterprise-scale responsibility position him well for both the brownfield US refinement and greenfield UK/Germany build components of this mandate.

candidates.flash_summary: Michael Keller is a Sr. Trade Compliance Specialist at Woodward, Inc., managing trade compliance for the entire aerospace segment of a $4B+ publicly traded company with 20+ years of experience.

scope_match.candidate_actual: US-based (Franklin, OH); current scope limited to US export compliance operations
scope_match.role_requirement: Global oversight across US, Mexico, UK, Germany, China, India

scope_match.candidate_actual: Aerospace and defense manufacturing (GE Aerospace — military and commercial); previously non-aerospace manufacturing (The Dupps Company)

key_criteria.name: Strong Export Compliance Experience
key_criteria.evidence: At **GE Aerospace**, serves as final checkpoint for all export-controlled shipments across military and commercial divisions, reviewing ITAR and EAR compliance and tracking licence usage and expiration. Does not have experience preparing or submitting DDTC licences — role is verification and tracking rather than origination.

key_criteria.name: Experience with US Laws and Regulations
key_criteria.evidence: Reviews ITAR and EAR controlled shipments daily at **GE Aerospace** and tracks DDTC licence compliance. Hands-on regulatory experience relatively recent (1.5 years); prior 8.5 years at **The Dupps Company** involved no military controls or ITAR requirements. Still developing depth beyond ITAR/EAR basics.

key_criteria.name: Aerospace and Defense Experience
key_criteria.evidence: Aerospace and defence experience limited to 1.5 years at **GE Aerospace**, where she stated military compliance knowledge "was new" and required learning from scratch. Prior 8.5 years at **The Dupps Company** involved non-aerospace manufacturing with no licence or military control requirements.

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work Cross-Functionally and Influence Across a Global Business
key_criteria.evidence: Built strong stakeholder relationships at **The Dupps Company** including customers, brokers, and freight forwarders for US-Brazil operations. At **GE Aerospace**, works within established process frameworks rather than influencing stakeholders to adopt new approaches. Limited experience collaborating across regions on regulatory matters.

key_criteria.name: Ability to Work in a Hands-On Entrepreneurial Environment
key_criteria.evidence: Operated as sole trade compliance authority for 8.5 years at **The Dupps Company** with complete autonomy — was the "go-to person for anything trade compliance." Proactively identified improvement needs and self-directed learning. Explicitly misses the impact and freedom of a smaller environment compared to **GE Aerospace**.

compensation.expected_total: Not explicitly stated; confirmed role would offer salary increase from current $135K total
compensation.flexibility: Candidate confirmed compensation expectations are within range offered and expressed openness to the opportunity, indicating flexibility

our_take.text: We believe Vivian's entrepreneurial drive and program-building instincts are genuine strengths, but the international regulatory gap she herself identified is a fundamental mismatch for a role requiring UK/Germany program leadership from the outset. Her aerospace depth is too recent to provide the industry credibility Doncasters needs across global stakeholders.

candidates.flash_summary: Vivian Imamura-Baker is Lead Specialist - International Trade Compliance at GE Aerospace, with 8.5 years building trade compliance programs at The Dupps Company before joining GE.
